title: Best Endocrinologist in Dwarka for Thyroid, Hormone and Diabetes
heading: Dr Aprajita Pradhan
description: Dr Aprajita Pradhan is the best endocrinologist in Dwarka for expert care and personalized treatment of thyroid, hormonal imbalances, and diabetes.
text:

Book an Appointment Welcome to Vedanta Super Speciality Clinic Reversal of type 2 Diabetes and Prediabetes - it is possible Book an Appointment 12 Years of work expeirece Best Endocrinologist in Dwarka, Delhi Dr Aprajita Pradhan Dr Aprajita Pradhan, an internationally trained endocrinologist, started her academic journey by completing her MBBS and MD (Internal Medicine) from NHL Municipal Medical College Ahmedabad, Gujarat University. She went on to work at the prestigious CMC, Vellore. She is one of the few Indians to receive training in clinical endocrinology at the prestigious Queen Mary University (St Bartholomew Hospital), London, which is one of the best international institutes providing training in endocrinology, worldwide. Here her work revolved around endocrinopathies associated with monoclonal antibodies. After that, she decided to return to her home country, where she completed her subspecialisation training: DNB in Endocrinology from the prestigious Medanta-The Medicity, Gurgaon. She brings her National and International experience in managing adult and paediatric endocrine diseases to provide comprehensive patient care. Her researches include work on Monoclonal Antibodies related to Endocrinopathies and NAFLD in Diabetes Mellitus. She has received awards and accolades for her research work. She is currently the director of Vedanta super specialty clinic, Dwarka where she gives consultation on all endocrine-related conditions. She is also a Consultant endocrinologist at Venkateshwar hospital, Dwarka New Delhi. Read More Change everything We’re here to help you At Vedanta superspeciality clinic, we strive to provide you with a comprehensive and personalised care plan, by combining best international endocrinological care standards with personalised nutritional planning, hormone correction and more. 
Individualized care and medical assessment through humanized care, while focussing on your overall health and well-being. Consultations In the first appointment with the consultant endocrinologist, your doctor shall work with you. You will be asked many questions, and you will undergo a thorough and comprehensive medical history and examination. Your anthropometric assessment will also be done, and serially monitored at subsequent visits. All your complaints will be reviewed, and shall be a review of past medical records and investigations. Hence, you are recommended to get all your medical records, investigations as you feel appropriate on your first visit. Accordingly appropriate diagnostic investigations (if needed) maybe planned, and a comprehensive personalized treatment regimen shall be planned and formulated, together with you. You are requested to stick to the regimen strictly , and may be needed to come for follow up as per your clinical requirements. Diagnostics Precise diagnoses based on clinical history, laboratory and imaging tests, with all the knowledge and experience of the specialist in endocrinology, shall be provided to you. Treatments Safe, effective and up-to-date treatments with all the guarantee of professional follow-up, with transparency and confidentiality between doctor and patient. Thanks Vedanta Clinic Area of Expertise Obesity Obesity is a multifactorial disease and the control of environmental and hormonal factors is necessary to combat it. Excess weight can be linked to the person's genetic heritage, bad lifestyle habits or, for example, endocrine dysfunctions. The treatment guided by the endocrinologist avoids associated diseases, such as diabetes, in addition to preventing a series of complications, such as cardiovascular and orthopedic ones. Diabetes Diabetes is a disease that is caused by insulin malfunctioning in your body or its scarce production. Insulin is an important hormone that regulates glucose in our blood and helps replenish energy for our body. Some specific symptoms (excessive thirst, diuresis and hunger) and tests that point to excess glucose are indications that should be investigated by a specialist. If you are overweight, relatives with diabetes, hypertension or blood fat changes, it is important to follow up with an endocrinologist. Thyroid Diseases It is a fundamental gland for the proper functioning of organs and important functions of the body. Thyroid diseases are related to changes in its functions, by releasing hormones in excess (hyperthyroidism) or in insufficient quantity (hypothyroidism). Nodules or swelling of the neck; insomnia and changes in bowel rhythm; nervousness; heart acceleration; weight gain or loss; and excess heat or cold can reveal thyroid disorders. Osteoporosis It develops from genetic, nutritional and hormonal factors, as well as from diseases that compromise the skeleton and the chronic use of certain medications. Fractures are the end result of this disease that can go unnoticed for several years. An endocrinologist will be able to diagnose and indicate the most appropriate treatment. Testosterone deficiency During aging, there is a gradual decrease in testosterone levels. This can lead to the need for hormone replacement in a number of men, which must be evaluated by an endocrinologist. The most common symptoms that may suggest a decrease in hormone production are tiredness, decreased muscle strength, erectile dysfunction and reduced sexual interest. Cholesterol and Triglyceride Changes Life habits, wrong diet, genetic inheritance and some diseases can lead to increased cholesterol and triglycerides in adults and children. Blood tests can point to changes in levels and treatment by an endocrinologist will reduce the risk of possible cardiovascular complications, especially the risk of heart attack and stroke. Healthy weight loss Healthy weight loss consists of eating based on a balanced diet, with the intake of all food groups, maintaining certain limits, in addition to the practice of physical activities. In some cases, controlling your diet and performing physical activities may not be enough. The endocrinologist is able to check the multiple factors that regulate metabolism throughout the body to point out possible treatments focused on maintaining health in the first place. Polycystic Ovary Syndrome It is an endocrinological disease characterized by the formation of cysts in the ovaries that cause them to increase in size. PCOS is characterized by irregular menstruation, the occurrence of acne and the production of testosterone (male hormone). In extreme situations, it can lead to obesity and infertility. Puberty Disorders Children who develop breasts, testicles or pubic hair very early have some type of hormonal disorder and it is necessary to assess the origin of the problem. Adolescents who do not develop these characteristics also need evaluation and follow-up regarding possible hormonal dysfunction. Diseases of the Adrenal Gland Among the diseases associated with disturbances in the production of hormones from the adrenal or adrenal gland are Addison's Disease, Cushing's Syndrome and Pheochromocytoma. Reddish stretch marks, weight gain, high or low blood pressure, acne, excessive hair, precocious puberty and skin darkening are some symptoms associated with adrenal gland problems. Pituitary Diseases Caused by deficiency or excess gland function, one of the most common problems occurs when a tumor appears, usually benign. Among the most common symptoms are changes in vision, headaches, menstrual irregularities, sexual dysfunction. The treatment may eventually involve a surgical procedure or just the regulation of deficient or excess hormones with medication. Menopause and Hormone Replacement It usually occurs between the ages of 45 and 55 and is confirmed 1 year after the last menstrual period. Menstruation stops because the ovaries stop producing hormones. It marks the end of a woman's reproductive years and is also known as "ovarian failure". The endocrinologist is able to indicate the need for adequate hormone replacement, made with hormones identical to those produced by the woman herself, which alleviates the discomfort and risks of this phase.
